description: Ensure that adding and removing reactions on comments requires user authentication on both frontend and backend

tasks.task: Verify and document that reaction endpoints in src/comments/comments.controller.ts are protected with @UseGuards(AuthGuard). The POST /comments/:commentId/reactions and DELETE /comments/:commentId/reactions endpoints should already have the AuthGuard decorator. If not present, add @UseGuards(AuthGuard) to these endpoints. Also ensure the reaction data uses req.user from the authenticated request rather than trusting client-provided user data.

In src/components/CommentSection.tsx, ensure the React button and ReactionPicker component are only rendered when user is authenticated (user !== null). Add a user authentication check at the start of handleReactionToggle function that returns early or shows a login prompt if user is null. The reaction toggle buttons should be disabled/hidden for unauthenticated users.

example_frontend: Navigate to a blog post with comments while not logged in - verify the 'React' button is NOT visible on any comments
Log in, navigate to a post with comments - verify the 'React' button IS visible on comments
Click React button while logged in, select an emoji - verify reaction is added successfully
Click the same emoji again - verify reaction is removed (toggle behavior)
Log out and verify the React buttons disappear from all comments
